titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Cadmus Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis, Jessica Tai, and Timothy G. Young

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 34 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 1,730 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 31, 2024 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Paul Cadmus papers, which span from 1812-2019, contain personal papers and files, correspondence, photographs, artwork, papers of others, artwork, address books, printed matter, and other materials that document the life and work of Paul Cadmus.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 95 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven series: I: Correspondence, 1920-2009. II: Personal papers, 1812-2018. III: Paul Cadmus files, 1930-2012. IV: Photographs, 1850-2010. V: Papers of others, 1890-2019. VI: Printed matter, 1930-2009. VII: Artwork, undated.

bioghist.head: Paul Cadmus
bioghist.p: Paul Cadmus was born on December 17, 1904, to artist parents Egbert Cadmus and Maria Latasa. His sister, Fidelma, was born December 11, 1906.

Paul Cadmus studied drawing and printmaking at the National Academy of Design from 1919 to 1926. While employed as a commercial layout artist in the late 1920s, Cadmus enrolled in classes at the Art Students League where he met fellow student Jared French (1905-1988). The two became romantically involved and began to focus on careers in fine art.

In 1934, Cadmus began working with the Public Works of Art Project, which sponsored a traveling exhibition featuring his painting The Fleet's In! a work that generated a great amount of response from viewers and raised Cadmus's public profile. His work in the late 1930s expanded to murals, solo exhibitions, and designs for ballets.

Jared French married the painter Margaret Hoenig (1906-1998) in 1937. French continued his relationship with Paul Cadmus and the three worked and lived in proximity for many years. Their photographic work, focused on images taken at the Hoenig family property in Saltaire, Fire Island, NY was attributed to PaJaMa, a portmanteau formed from PAul, JAred, and MArgaret.

Over the following decades, the lives of the three painters diverged, with Jared French moving semi-permanently to Italy and Margaret French splitting her time between Europe and the United States. Paul Cadmus lived in New York City for much of this period, before moving to Weston, CT in the 1970s in a house built on the property of his brother-in-law, Lincoln Kirstein. (Kirstein and Fidelma Cadmus having married in 1941.) In 1965, Cadmus met Jon F. Anderson, an actor and cabaret artist, who became his life partner and muse for the next 34 years.
The 1980s and 1990s saw a resurgence for Paul Cadmus through monographs dedicated to his work, exhibitions, personal appearances in advertising campaigns, and a documentary feature.

Paul Cadmus died on December 12, 1999. Jon F. Anderson, who had later married Philis Raskind, passed away on October 21, 2018. Jared French died January 8, 1988. Margaret French died November 18, 1998.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Cadmus Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, writings, artwork, printed material, photographs, and other papers created by or related to the life and work of Paul Cadmus. The papers document Cadmus's lengthy career; his art practice creating paintings, drawings, murals, and prints; as well as his personal life and relationships with many notable artists and writers of the 20th century.

The lives and work of many friends, family, and associates are also documented in the papers. Of note are Fidelma Cadmus (Paul's sister) Lincoln Kirstein (his brother-in-law) artist and partner Jared French, artist and friend Margaret French, and Paul Cadmus's long-time partner, Jon F. Anderson.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Cadmus Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the auhors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: A majority of materials in this series have been arranged to highlight long-term and key correspondents by name (in separate folders or in alphabetic groups). Remaining correspondence was sorted into broad topical or chronological groupings. Additional letters by named correspondents may be found in other groupings, as item-level sorting was not done within this series.
Correspondence can also be found in Series III and Series V.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence to and from Paul Cadmus's friends, family members, collaborators, researchers, museums, galleries, and a wide variety of individuals and organizations.

did.unittitle: Correspondence written by Cadmus with related materials
American Academy of Arts and Letters photograph with related correspondence and mailing tube

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials largely maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials related to the life and work of Paul Cadmus, including records of exhibitions and sales of artworks, address and appointment books, notebooks, writings by and about Paul Cadmus, writings by friends and colleagues, childhood drawings by Paul and Fidelma Cadmus (his sister), and personal documents.

did.unittitle: Address books, appointment books, and other personal papers--Jon F. Anderson
Address books, appointment books, and other personal papers--Jon F. Anderson
Reading list made by Katherine Anne Porter
Blueprints for underground installation in Weston, CT home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings by Donald Windham, George Platt Lynes, Glenway Wescott, Bridget Chisholm, and others.

did.unittitle: Clippings, photographs, and other scrapbook materials
Engraved copper plate by George Cruickshank
Engraved copper plate by Paul Cadmus
Publicity kit for "Paul Cadmus: Enfant Terrible at 80"
Notebook--Deadly sins and other pictures to be
Writings about Cadmus--dissertation by Philip Eliasoph
Writings about Cadmus--dissertation by Richard Meyer
Writings about Cadmus--dissertation by Anthony J. Morris
Writings about Cadmus--dissertation by Gary Deb Sweeney
Writings about Cadmus--catalog raisonne drafts by Philip Eliasoph
Childhood drawings by Paul and Fidelma Cadmus
Childhood drawings by Paul and Fidelma Cadmus
Childhood drawings by Paul and Fidelma Cadmus

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a mix of correspondence, notes, and clippings by and about persons and subjects. Folders retained titles present at the time of acquisition from the Cadmus/Anderson Estate.

did.unittitle: Correspondence with Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence with Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence with Jared and Margaret French

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Materials related to closing of Midtown Payson Galleries
Materials related to false Cadmus sold by Berryhill Galleries
Bread and Roses pictures and negatives
Materials related to art sales other than Midtown
Writings and notes by Paul Cadmus
Clippings related to Jon Anderson's family
Materials related to Paul Cadmus's death and estate
Stephen May letter and related materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains images created by PaJaMa--a collaborative artistic endeavor between Paul Cadmus, Jared French, and Margaret French. Other photographs in the series include personal images by Cadmus and others documenting travel, social gatherings, and portraits. The series contains photographs from Jared French and Margaret French, including childhood photographs, portraits, school portraits, travel, and recreation. Also included in this series are photographs documenting artwork by Cadmus.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: I. PaJaMa, 1930 - 1981. II. Personal photographs, 1860 - 2009. III. Jared French and Margaret Hoening French family photographs, 1850 - 1988. IV. Photographs of artwork, 1929 - 2010.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Nitrate negatives that were found upon receipt of the collection were removed in accordance with the policy that the Library does not retain nitrate negatives, nor negatives that may be nitrate. As of October 2022, the negatives are in the process of being digitized, and the files will be available to consult following completion of digitization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs created by PaJaMa--a collaborative artistic endeavor between Paul Cadmus, Jared French, and Margaret French. The PaJaMa images consist of prints, color slides, contact sheets, and photograph boards containing image assemblages.

descriptivenote.p: Photograph index and notes

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards C, D, E, F, G and box

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards C, D, E, F, G and box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of figures on the beach, on structures, and in interiors. Photographs are taken in locations including Mulhockaway and Princeton, NJ. Includes photographs of John Butler, Paul Cadmus, Jared French, Margaret French, Francis Burton Harrison Jr, José Martínez, Fred Melton, Bernard Perlin, Tennessee Williams, and Donald Windham.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 101 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards C, D, P

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards C, D, P

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes additional C and D as well as P boards consisting of photographs of figures on the beach, and in interiors. Photographs are taken in locations including Florence, Italy. Includes photographs of Margaret French and Jared French.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 102 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards H, I, J and box

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards H, I, J and box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of figures on the beach and posed in interiors. Photographs are taken in locations including East Hampton, Fire Island, Monroe, Nantucket, Palisades Park, and Provincetown. Includes photographs of Paul Cadmus, Sandy Campbell, Margaret French, Christopher Isherwood, George Tooker, and Donald Windham.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 104 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards K, L and box

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards K, L and box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of boardwalk scenes, figures on the beach, figure studies, gravestones, portraits, and photographs of Edward Hopper.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 103 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards M and box

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards M and box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of artwork, architecture, cemeteries, figures on the beach, and sculptures. Photographs are taken in locations including Fire Island, Italy, and New York. Includes photographs of George Tooker.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 105 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

did.unittitle: PaJaMa photograph boards N, Q and box

descriptivenote.p: PaJaMa photograph boards N, Q and box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of architecture, artwork, beach scenes, nudes, portraits, and sculptures. Photographs are taken in locations including Amalfi, Barcelona, Basel, Beaulieu, Bologna, Capri, Copenhagen, Fire Island, Freiburg im Breisgau, Genoa, Monaco, Munich, Oslo, Rome, and Venice. Includes photographs of Paul Cadmus and Donald Windham.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As of November 2022, PaJaMa photographs in box 106 are undergoing conservation and digitization work. This box will be available to consult following completion of this work.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Nitrate negatives that were found upon receipt of the collection were removed in accordance with the policy that the Library does not retain nitrate negatives, nor negatives that may be nitrate. As of October 2022, the negatives are in the process of being digitized, and the files will be available to consult following completion of digitization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album contains images of Charles Daugherty, Karl Free, Jared French, Margaret French, Lincoln Kirstein, Edward Lanning, George Platt Lynes, Fred Melton, Wilbur Pippin, Forrest Thayer, and Monroe Wheeler. Locations include Fire Island. Photographs are taken by Jared French, Margaret French, and Paul Cadmus (PaJaMa).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of George Platt Lynes.

descriptivenote.p: Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One photograph contains the inscription "GPL" referring to the artist George Platt Lynes.

scopecontent.p: Slides featuring images created by PaJaMa, as well as documentation of Cadmus' artwork, and photographs of Jon Anderson and Paul Cadmus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph with the inscription, "GP Lynes- his camera" referring to the artist George Platt Lynes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs of family and friends, vacations, travel, nudes, portraits, theater performances, and social events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged chronologically, with portraits of individuals located alphabetically at the end of the subseries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of commercially produced carte-de-visite. Subjects include royalty, classical figure sculptures, and locations including Italy, Greece, and Spain.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains portraits of Paul Cadmus, Cadmus family members, Ben-Hur Baz, Antonio Carluccio, Lloyd Goff, Lincoln Kirstein, Alfonso A. Ossorio, Wilbur Pippin, George Tooker, Monroe Wheeler, and Donald Windham.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Nitrate negatives that were found upon receipt of the collection were removed in accordance with the policy that the Library does not retain nitrate negatives, nor negatives that may be nitrate. As of October 2022, the negatives are in the process of being digitized, and the files will be available to consult following completion of digitization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains family photographs from Jared French and Margaret Hoening French, including childhood photographs, portraits, school portraits, travel, and recreation. Includes 35mm negatives with original canisters.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Nitrate negatives that were found upon receipt of the collection were removed in accordance with the policy that the Library does not retain nitrate negatives, nor negatives that may be nitrate. As of October 2022, the negatives are in the process of being digitized, and the files will be available to consult following completion of digitization.

descriptivenote.p: Negatives: portraits, snapshots, travel
Negatives: portraits, snapshots, travel

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Nitrate negatives that were found upon receipt of the collection were removed in accordance with the policy that the Library does not retain nitrate negatives, nor negatives that may be nitrate. As of October 2022, the negatives are in the process of being digitized, and the files will be available to consult following completion of digitization.

did.unittitle: Photographs: group portraits, snapshots, travel, Zermatt, Switzerland
Photographs: construction, exteriors, group portraits, snapshots
Photographs: contact sheets, farming, snapshots, tree work
Two photographs featuring a Native American woman and child on a horse
Canister and negatives 2 "J's head, Spring Street, [Abriegeln?], Mr. Gilett's house": house exteriors, Jared French portraits, unidentified groups of people
Canister and negatives 3 "'57 #3 P2B moving, house, pond"
Canister and negatives 4 "JT/E house, '57": unidentified individuals outside house and in field
Negatives 6 "'58 J Bugbee grandchild, Ascutney views, [Howard's?] barn, J. digging, closeup, our barn": construction, Jared French portraits
Photographs: Italy, Santa Fe, Taos, snapshots, travel, Margaret French

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: As of October 2022, some materials in this folder are currently undergoing conservation treatment. Complete-JF 2/20/2024

did.unittitle: Canister and negatives 7 "1959 #9": outdoor landscapes, construction
Contact sheet 10: house construction, water recreation, circus performance
Four unlabeled rolls of 35mm negatives 11: Jared French portraits, circus performance, housing construction, recreation, outdoor landscapes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains photographs documenting artwork by Cadmus as well as other artists.

did.unittitle: Photographs of artwork by other artists
Color glass slides of "The Seven Deadly Sins"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Previously housed in George Platt Lynes envelopes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into four subseries: I. Jared French papers, 1925-1989. II. Margaret French papers, 1890-1998. III. Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French, 1940-1999. IV. Jon Anderson papers, 1918-2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the papers of Jared French, Margaret French, and Jon Anderson. Each subseries contains personal papers and correspondence. For both Jared French and Margaret French, writings are also included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the papers of Jared French including personal papers writings, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Notes and documentation related to art and taxes
Clippings, correspondence, and other documentation related to Jared French murals
Writings—Translation of "Corydon" by Andre Gide with annotations from Gide

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the papers of Margaret French including personal papers, writings, and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Notes and other materials related to French's correspondence
Correspondence and materials related to doctors and medical care
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence between Jared and Margaret French.

did.unittitle: Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence between Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence from Fidelma Cadmus to Margaret French
Correspondence from Mary Flagler Cary to Margaret French
Correspondence from William Christopher to Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence from Jack Dunphy to Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence from E. M. Forster to Margaret French
Correspondence from Jo Hopper to Jared French
Correspondence from Lincoln Kirstein to Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence from George Platt Lynes and Russell Lynes to Jared and Margaret French
Envelope from Marianne Moore to Jared French
Correspondence from Schulte's bookstore to Jared French
Correspondence from Osbert Sitwell to Margaret French
Correspondence from George Tooker to Jared and Margaret French
Correspondence from Glenway Wescott and Monroe Wheeler to Jared and Margaret French

Correspondence to and from Jared French
Correspondence related to property in Hartland, VT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the papers of Jon Anderson including personal papers and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Clippings and other papers related to Paul Cadmus

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains printed matter including newspapers, magazines, newsletters, clippings, catalogs, and programs that document the life and work of Paul Cadmus and his artistic community.

did.unittitle: Academic and university programs and publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains artwork depicting Paul Cadmus including a photographic portrait and two sculptural busts.
